item.title: U.S. Senate sources say Digital Asset Market Clarity Act floor vote is expected during the week of July 20.
SEC plans to propose three crypto rules in July covering token offerings, broker-dealer custody, and trading venue registration.
U.S. regulators face a July 18 deadline to finalize GENIUS Act implementing rules governing domestic stablecoin issuers.
Circle received final OCC approval on July 10 to establish Circle National Trust, a federally chartered bank for USDC custody.
Sony Bank's Connectia Trust won conditional OCC approval on July 9 to form a $40M-capitalized U.S. stablecoin trust bank.
BlackRock's IBIT drew $209M in a single session on July 6, leading $265M in total U.S. spot Bitcoin ETF inflows.
Australia's AML/CTF Travel Rule for crypto took full effect July 1, requiring identity and wallet checks on all exchange transfers.
OCC confirmed national banks may custody crypto assets, hold stablecoin reserves, and join blockchain networks without prior approval.
A malicious governance proposal drained an estimated $20M in BONK from BonkDAO's treasury on July 7.
Summer.fi halted Lazy Summer Protocol vaults on July 6 after a $6M flash loan exploit manipulated USDC vault accounting.
An exploit drained approximately 16M ADA from 374 Cardano wallets on July 9, disrupting on-chain governance participants.
JPMorgan warned U.S. lawmakers that rushed crypto market structure legislation could create regulatory loopholes as CLARITY Act advances.
EU's MiCA transitional period closed July 1, requiring all crypto asset service providers in the EU to hold valid authorization.
SEC is targeting a 20-year-old trading system rule to allow broker-dealers to trade tokenized securities on blockchain platforms.
